headline: Molina Healthcare (MOH) Is Up 6.2% After Higher 2027 MA Rates And Klarman Stake - Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services recently finalized higher 2027 Medicare Advantage payment rates, which has improved sentiment toward government-focused health insurers such as Molina Healthcare, ahead of its upcoming first-quarter 2026 earnings release, conference call, and investor day. Investor attention has also been drawn by discussions around Molina Healthcare’s valuation and margins and the opening of a new position by billionaire investor Seth Klarman, underscoring how...

headline: Assessing Molina Healthcare (MOH) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness And Undervaluation Narrative
summary: Molina Healthcare (MOH) is drawing investor attention after a prolonged share-price slide, with the stock down over the past month and past 3 months despite reporting US$43.6b in revenue and US$472m in net income. See our latest analysis for Molina Healthcare. At a share price of US$141.53, Molina Healthcare has seen short term momentum pick up with a 7 day share price return of 6.17%. However, the 1 year total shareholder return of 57.2% and 5 year total shareholder return of 41.62%...

headline: Did Oscar Health’s CEO Just Cast a $12 Million Vote of Confidence?
summary: Investing legend Peter Lynch once noted that insiders sell for any number of reasons, but they buy for only one — they think the company’s stock price will rise. The CEO of beleaguered health insurer Oscar Health (NYSE:OSCR) just made a big purchase of company stock, suggesting a vote of confidence in Oscar, whose shares ... Did Oscar Health’s CEO Just Cast a $12 Million Vote of Confidence?
